status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 5 / IDENTITY OR PRODUCT-EQUIVALENCE BLOCK ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
mechanism_summary: Identity-dependent AMPK and adenosine-regulation research. The term AICAR is used inconsistently for AICA riboside/acadesine and the intracellular phosphorylated nucleotide ZMP.
evidence_maturity: Human acadesine studies exist in cardiac-surgery settings, including a large negative randomised trial. Exercise-mimetic and metabolic claims are largely preclinical and must not be transferred without exact chemical identity.
regulatory_headline: CHEMICAL FORM AMBIGUOUS / NO CURRENT GENERAL THERAPEUTIC APPROVAL CLAIM / CURRENT ANTI-DOPING CONTROL REQUIRED
closure_class: AICA RIBOSIDE / ACADESINE VERSUS PHOSPHORYLATED AICAR IDENTITY
closure_state: PARTIAL CLOSURE — HUMAN ACADESINE DEVELOPMENT AND CURRENT ANTI-DOPING STATUS IDENTIFIED; SUPPLIER CHEMICAL FORM, SALT, GRADE AND ROUTE OPEN
authoritative_product_boundary: The label AICAR is used ambiguously for AICA riboside/acadesine and the intracellular phosphorylated nucleotide AICAR/ZMP. The RED-CABG phase 3 acadesine programme was terminated and did not establish the supplier vial. The 2026 WADA list prohibits AICAR as an AMPK activator.
critical_closure_controls: Do not use AICAR as one unambiguous chemical name
Historical acadesine human evidence does not validate AR50 or AR100
The RED-CABG development programme does not create an exercise indication
AICAR remains prohibited under the current WADA metabolic-modulator control
No exercise-mimetic, performance, dosing, injection or reconstitution protocol
